title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Aluminum prices pulled back marginally to $3,606.96/ton (-0.33%) after recent four-year highs, due to early signals of restored vessel flows through the Strait of Hormuz. The market remains structurally tight from low LME inventories and Middle East supply constraints, including damage to smelting infrastructure in the UAE and Bahrain. Robust demand and high energy costs keep Chinese prices elevated at ¥24,658/ton.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3603 USD/T on April 24, 2026, down 0.46% from the previous day, though up 11.02% over the past month and 47.82% over the year. UK aluminum futures held above $3,600 per tonne near a four-year high amid Strait of Hormuz blockage threats to Middle Eastern supply. Prices sank to $3,550 from a $3,670 high as vessel flows resumed, with Iranian authorities allowing commercial navigation.

title: Price of Aluminum per Pound
summary: Live aluminum price stood at 1.6524 USD per pound (bid) on April 24, 2026, down 0.08%. Kitco provides spot prices from New York, London, Hong Kong, and Sydney markets. The update reflects the latest trading session amid ongoing market volatility.

title: Aluminium faces 'black swan' supply shock, Mercuria says
summary: Mercuria warns of a major aluminum supply shock due to Middle East conflict disrupting roughly 9% of global supply, leading to a potential deficit of at least two million tons by year-end. This assumes some smelter restarts if alumina flows improve via the Strait of Hormuz, but low global stocks leave limited buffers. China faces output limits, while the US and Europe have little idle capacity.

title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
summary: The US faces a severe aluminum supply crisis, with Bloomberg warning supplies could hit zero in under 30 days due to Middle East production disruptions and a 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum. This tariff, meant to protect domestic production, has instead threatened the manufacturing base amid lack of foreign sources. Bank of America notes the deficit could widen to 1.5 million tons if conditions persist.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock Raises Risks For Global Metals Markets
summary: StoneX analyst Natalie Scott-Gray discusses an aluminum supply shock driven by geopolitical risks, trade policy shifts, and energy prices reshaping global metals markets. Tightening aluminum supply is highlighted alongside tin disruptions and weakening copper demand as inventories build. The outlook emphasizes risks from prolonged Middle East disruptions.

title: FIMIGroup to Deliver Two New Heavy-Gauge Leveling and Cut-to-Length Lines to JSW Steel in India
summary: FIMIGroup announced it will deliver two new heavy-gauge leveling and cut-to-length lines to JSW Steel in India. This innovation enhances processing capabilities for heavy-gauge steel, supporting efficient production. The development aligns with advancements in steel technology for industrial applications.

title: Copper fell to 5.98 USD/Lbs on April 24, 2026
summary: Copper prices declined 1.57% to $5.98 per pound on April 24, 2026. Over the past month, copper has risen 8.06% and is up 23.55% year-to-date, showing overall strength despite the recent daily decline.

title: Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco announced that its El Teniente mine smelter is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact copper supply dynamics in the market.

title: Copper Futures Price Today
summary: The current price of copper futures stands at 6.0270, down from the previous close of 6.0815. This reflects recent market movements in copper trading.

title: Record Margins, Shrinking Supply: Why Capital Is Chasing Copper Right Now
summary: ING Group forecasts a 2026 refined copper deficit of 600,000 tons due to mine disruptions and tariff-driven stockpiling draining inventories. Taseko Mines has begun copper cathode production at its Florence Copper operation in Arizona, the first new greenfield facility in the US since 2008, aiming for 85 million pounds annually. Hudbay Minerals projects a 24% increase in consolidated copper production through 2028, driven by mine life extensions.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group aligned with illicit mineral activities in Congo and related mining firms. This action targets disruptions in mineral supply chains, potentially impacting copper production in the region. The sanctions aim to curb illegal mining operations.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto achieved a major milestone by producing the first copper cathode using its proprietary Nuton bioleaching technology after over 30 years of research and development. The technology, which relies on microorganisms grown on site, was deployed at Gunnison Copper's Johnson Camp mine and is expected to produce approximately 30,000 tonnes of refined copper over a four-year demonstration period with a mine-to-metal carbon footprint of 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper—the lowest in the U.S.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: This article examines copper's critical role in the clean energy transition, highlighting that the International Energy Agency projects copper demand for clean energy could nearly double by 2050 driven by electrification. The piece discusses how innovations and technologies are essential to meeting the growing demand for copper in sustainable energy infrastructure.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Global nickel prices edged down 0.60% to $18,135.31 per ton, with Chinese markets at ¥123,740 per ton. On April 24, 2026, nickel prices advanced 1.55% to $18,723.31/ton (¥127,996/ton in China), driven by tightening global supply, Indonesia’s revised HPM ore pricing policy, Weda Bay’s planned production halt, and projections of a structural market deficit. Demand is bolstered by optimism surrounding China's ultra-long special bond issuances.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures traded above $18,750, hovering close to a more than three-month high of $18,775, with the market consolidating after a strong rally. Nickel rose to 19,125 USD/T on April 24, 2026, up 1.86% from the previous day.

title: Indonesia nickel processing utilization to drop amid lower nickel ore
summary: Indonesia's nickel industry group FINI stated that 2026 nickel processing production capacity utilization could drop to as low as 70% due to a lower nickel ore production quota. This is a decline from around 90% last year, with nickel ore imports expected to surge. FINI chairman Arif Perdana Kusumah shared this with Reuters.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: The world's biggest nickel mine, PT Weda Bay Nickel in Indonesia, has been notified to cut its ore production quota to 12 million tons this year from 42 million tons in 2025. This move aims to boost global prices of nickel, Indonesia's key export commodity. The company intends to apply for a revision.
